data.description:
## Context

rebar's test suite drives the tool largely by SUBPROCESS — the CLI adapter shells out to `python -m rebar.cli`, and many exit-code/CLI tests do the same (≈245 test files use subprocess / spec_from_file_location). coverage.py's in-process tracer never sees that child-process execution, so the measured in-process coverage (**77%** on 2026-06-27, see docs/coverage.md) UNDERSTATES the true exercised fraction. Because of this, `fail_under` is held at a deliberately conservative 70 — it cannot honestly track real coverage while subprocess work is invisible.

Research (peers + coverage.py docs) summary:
- **Modern recipe is ~3 lines**: `[tool.coverage.run] patch = ["subprocess"]` (coverage.py >= 7.10; auto-enables `parallel = true`) plus a `coverage combine` step before the report. pytest-cov >= 7.0 removed its own subprocess support and defers to coverage.py.
- **rebar is the easy case**: its subprocesses run in the SAME environment as the tests (no throwaway venvs), so NO `[tool.coverage.paths]` remapping is needed (unlike pip/tox/virtualenv).
- **Not universal practice**: process-spawning tools (pip, tox, nox, virtualenv, coverage.py itself) measure subprocess coverage; many mature CLIs (pdm, hatch, pipx, pre-commit) accept the in-process undercount with a conservative floor — the current documented stance here.
- **Real gotchas for us**: the `coverage combine` step becomes mandatory (erase -> test -> combine -> report); per-process tracing overhead (mitigate with `core = "sysmon"` on 3.12+); and crucially, rebar has many CLI EXIT-CODE tests — a child that calls `os._exit()` or is SIGTERM-killed never flushes coverage data, so we likely need `patch = ["subprocess", "_exit"]` and/or `sigterm = true`, and should expect to chase some flakiness.

Priority P4: optional, forward-looking. docs/coverage.md already documents this under "Future: subprocess coverage". Pick up when raising the real coverage number becomes worth the combine-step + exit-code-flush complexity.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] CI measures the TRUE exercised coverage (subprocess child processes counted), and `fail_under` is raised to track that real number instead of the in-process undercount — with no new test flakiness.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `[tool.coverage.run] patch = ["subprocess"]` (or the classic COVERAGE_PROCESS_START + parallel recipe if needed) is enabled and instruments `python -m rebar.cli` children.
- [ ] The exit-code-test flush problem is handled (`patch` includes `_exit` and/or `sigterm = true`); the CLI exit-code suite stays green with no flakiness.
- [ ] The Makefile + CI coverage step run erase -> test -> `coverage combine` -> report in order (the combine step is wired so the report reads the merged parallel data).
- [ ] The reported coverage number measurably rises above the 77% in-process figure (subprocess work now counted); the delta is recorded in docs/coverage.md.
- [ ] `fail_under` is raised to a real guard under the new measured number; docs/coverage.md baseline + 'Future' section updated to reflect that subprocess coverage is now ON.
- [ ] Perf overhead is acceptable (consider `core = "sysmon"` on 3.12+); CI wall-clock regression is noted.
data.title: Subprocess coverage: measure CLI child-process execution so the floor tracks true coverage
